subject: D1 data aggregation pipeline on cabinet — reusable binary opportunity

body.context: The psychology-agent mesh runs monitoring via Cloudflare D1 (hrcb-db, 772 MB after 15 days). story_snapshots grows at ~58K rows/day, events at ~22K rows/day. D1 read costs already exceed free tier. Architecture decision resolved: hybrid model — D1 for short-term edge monitoring, local SQLite on cabinet for historical analytics.
body.request: Design and implement a D1-to-cabinet aggregation pipeline. The pipeline should run as a scheduled job on cabinet (Jenkins or cron), pull raw data from D1, aggregate into local SQLite, and enforce a retention window on D1 raw data.

pipeline_components: D1 data puller — authenticated read from Cloudflare D1 via API, extracting raw rows from story_snapshots and events tables
SQLite aggregator — daily rollup (avg/min/max/count per story per day for snapshots, daily counts by event type for events)
D1 retention enforcer — delete raw rows older than 7 days (story_snapshots) and 14 days (events) after confirming aggregation success
Jenkins pipeline definition — scheduled daily execution, health check reporting, failure alerting

schema_design.story_snapshots_daily: story_id, date, score_avg, score_min, score_max, snapshot_count, dimensions (JSON or separate columns per dimension)
schema_design.event_counts_daily: date, event_type, count, metadata_sample (first event's metadata as reference)
schema_design.note: Schema should accommodate future analytical queries — window functions, trend detection, anomaly flagging

infrastructure_facts.cabinet: Linux x86_64, Jenkins host, Go 1.24, Python 3, same LAN as chromabook, SSH access to chromabook and Hetzner
infrastructure_facts.d1_database: hrcb-db, Cloudflare account accessible via API token
infrastructure_facts.meshd: Running on chromabook, could expose cabinet SQLite via /kb/analytics route if aggregated data needs edge visibility

reusable_binary_opportunity.observation: The D1-to-SQLite pull-aggregate-purge pattern generalizes beyond this use case. Any project using D1 as a hot cache with local cold storage benefits from the same binary.
reusable_binary_opportunity.proposed_factoring: A standalone Go or Rust binary that accepts config (D1 API credentials, table names, aggregation SQL, retention window) and executes the pull-aggregate-purge cycle. The psychology-agent mesh consumes it via config; other projects consume it via their own config.
reusable_binary_opportunity.privacy_boundary: The binary itself contains no project-specific data, credentials, or domain logic. All specifics live in config files that remain in their respective private repos. The binary operates on table schemas it receives, not schemas it knows.
reusable_binary_opportunity.distribution: Could ship as a public GitHub release (MIT/Apache 2.0) or remain internal. The factoring decision belongs to claude-control.

constraints: No data loss — aggregation must complete and verify before raw data purge
Idempotent — safe to re-run if a previous execution partially completed
Observable — logs aggregation counts, purge counts, and any errors to stdout (Jenkins captures)
License-compatible — MIT or Apache 2.0 only (psychology-agent dependency policy)

action_gate.gate_condition: claude-control reviews scope and responds with implementation plan or counter-proposal

epistemic_flags: D1 API rate limits not yet characterized — aggregation batch size may need tuning
story_snapshots schema inferred from query patterns, not direct table inspection — verify column names before building puller
Cabinet disk capacity not audited — confirm sufficient space for unbounded SQLite growth
